investigated: Pipeline calibration (seed-000), PEAD for mega-cap S&P 500 stocks with >10% EPS beats (seed-001), S&P 500 index addition effect (seed-002), FDA PDUFA decision literature review (seed-003)
findings: PIPELINE: Calibrated successfully - META Feb 2023 (+21.82% measured vs +23% actual), GOOGL Oct 2022 (-8.38% measured vs -9% actual), NVDA Feb 2024 (+14.33% measured vs +16% actual). All match expected direction and magnitude. PEAD DEAD END: No post-earnings drift for mega-cap S&P 500 with >10% EPS beats. Discovery set (2021-2022, n=20): avg 1d abnormal = +1.05%, positive rate = 45% (below random!), 1d→5d drift = -0.94% (mean reversion). Financials (GS/MS 2020-2021, n=13): avg 1d = +0.40%, drift = -1.50%. Not significant. 2023-2024 tech shows strong returns (+8.37%) but confounded by AI/META theme. INDEX ADDITION EFFECT CONFIRMED: Discovery (2020-2022, n=15): avg 1d abnormal = +4.09%, 5d = +5.52%, 73% positive rate. Validation (2023-2024, n=10): avg 1d = +8.05%, 5d = +10.23%, 100% positive rate. Both pass multiple testing. Hypothesis 061ae3a8 created (confidence 9/10). FDA LITERATURE: Effect size-dependent - large pharma approval near-zero (+0.4%), small biotech +8-11% vs pharma. Rejection (CRL) = -4.3% to -9.2%, asymmetrically larger. Continued drift for 2+ weeks post-rejection.
surprised_by: Two big surprises: (1) PEAD showed NEGATIVE abnormal returns on day 1 for large-cap beats (45% positive rate, below random). Classic 'sell the news' - these beats were fully priced in or exceeded by whisper numbers. (2) S&P 500 addition effect is CLEARLY ALIVE in 2020-2024 despite Greenwood & Sammon (2025 JF) claiming it disappeared by 2020. Validation set actually showed STRONGER effect than discovery (100% positive rate vs 73%). The growth of passive investing may be re-amplifying the forced buying effect. Need to monitor whether next non-thematic addition also shows the pattern.
next_step: Morning session: check if any March 2026 S&P 500 announcement was missed (Q1 rebalance may have just occurred). Research session: backtest FDA CRL rejection for small biotech (including delisted companies), test S&P 500 deletion effect, test post-effective-date reversal for additions. When next S&P 500 addition announced, place $5000 paper trade immediately.

investigated:
(1) March 2026 S&P 500 additions live validation (VRT, LITE, COHR, SATS), 
(2) S&P 500 deletion effect backtest (N=21, 2021-2026),
(3) S&P 500 post-effective-date reversal test (N=24, 2020-2024),
(4) FDA small biotech CRL (Complete Response Letter) backtest (N=8 measured + 4 delisted, 2018-2025)
findings:
MARCH 2026 LIVE VALIDATION: All 4 S&P 500 additions positive (VRT +8.46%, LITE +13.85%, COHR +6.17%, SATS +2.59%), avg +7.77% 1d abnormal. 5-day window already passed (Mar 9-13). Confirms hypothesis 061ae3a8.

DELETION EFFECT: Dead end. 1d avg=-0.44% (p=0.59), positive_rate=52%. Validation set 2024-2026: 1d avg=+0.27% (POSITIVE!), 3d passes Bonferroni within full set but not in OOS. Many deleted stocks RISE on Day 1 as contrarian buyers offset index fund selling. Selection bias is severe.

POST-EFFECTIVE REVERSAL: NOT present. 1d avg=-0.61% (p=0.36), 20d avg=+1.16% (p=0.70). Addition gains are PERMANENT (investor recognition effect). No need to sell before effective date.

FDA CRL BACKTEST: Generic CRL short = dead end. CMC-only CRLs (AXSM +4.8%, SRRK +4.1%) show POSITIVE 1d returns. Clinical CRLs show high variance: OTLK -81%, APLT -77% vs ITRM +18%, NERV +1.2%. Survivorship bias severe: 4/12 tickers delisted (SESN -80% known, ATNX -21% known, ICPT unknown, CORV -1.5%). True effect MORE negative than measured. Dead end for generic CRL short. Refined hypothesis (single-asset + clinical CRL) queued for future research.
surprised_by:
1. S&P 500 DELETION effect: Day 1 is essentially FLAT (52% positive, avg -0.44%). Many stocks go UP on deletion announcement as contrarian buyers see 'oversold' stocks. The deletion creates a 3-day negative drift but not a reliable 1-day short. The 'obvious' short (index forced selling) doesn't work on Day 1 because the market correctly anticipates buyer interest.

2. CMC-only FDA CRL causes POSITIVE stock reactions (AXSM +4.8%, SRRK +4.1%). This makes fundamental sense: a manufacturing fix is temporary, but the market's ability to distinguish CMC from clinical CRLs in real-time is impressive. Smaller biotechs with clinical issues are not this lucky.

3. The MARCH 2026 additions show the effect is getting STRONGER, not weaker: LITE +13.85%, average +7.77% vs our prior validation set average of +8.05%. The effect was supposed to have disappeared by 2020 (Greenwood & Sammon 2025) but 2026 data shows it very much alive.
next_step: Test mid-cap PEAD (task 9845a59d). Start with S&P 500 components with $5-30B market cap that beat EPS by >10%, find 20+ events from 2020-2024. This is the next highest-priority quantitative test. Also: look for alternative data sources for delisted biotech tickers.

investigated: Insider buying cluster hypothesis feasibility: (1) SEC EDGAR Form 4 data availability and access methods, (2) recent academic evidence on whether signal persists in 2020-2024, (3) specific APIs and tools available, (4) end-to-end pipeline test
findings: FEASIBILITY CONFIRMED. (1) DATA: SEC provides free quarterly bulk ZIP downloads at https://www.sec.gov/files/structureddata/data/insider-transactions-data-sets/{YEAR}q{Q}_form345.zip. Files: NONDERIV_TRANS.tsv has TRANS_CODE (P=open market purchase), TRANS_SHARES, TRANS_PRICEPERSHARE, TRANS_DATE. SUBMISSION.tsv has ISSUERTRADINGSYMBOL (ticker). REPORTINGOWNER.tsv has RPTOWNER_RELATIONSHIP (Director/Officer). Q4 2024 ZIP = 8.5MB compressed, ~55MB uncompressed. URL pattern confirmed working (HTTP 200). (2) PIPELINE TEST: Q4 2024 data yielded 1,330 qualified purchases (code P, >$50K, director/officer) and 487 cluster events in one quarter. Sample: BHVN had 2 insiders each buying $999,970 on same day. (3) TOOLS: edgartools (pip install edgartools, MIT license, free, no API key). EFTS full-text search API works with User-Agent header. (4) ACADEMIC: Kang/Kim/Wang 2018 (1986-2016): cluster purchases 3.8% abnormal/21d vs 2.0% non-cluster. Alldredge/Blank 2017: 2.1% monthly abnormal for clustered. Lakonishok/Lee 2001: 7-8% annual outperformance. SIGNAL DECAY: 2024 study shows raw individual insider signal vanishes with realistic position sizing, negatively correlated with liquidity. CLUSTER-specific decay not studied. 2025 SSRN paper 'Death of Insider Trading Alpha' - most returns occur before public disclosure (i.e., pre-filing). Microcap insider purchase signal persists (2018-2024, n=17,237): 6.3% abnormal CAR for purchases after >10% price surge.
surprised_by: The SEC bulk data is far more accessible than expected - free, structured TSV format, quarterly ZIPs, no API key needed. The EFTS full-text search API also works with a simple User-Agent header and returns 534+ Form 4 filings per day. The total cluster event count of 487 per quarter (likely 1,500-2,000 per year after filtering) is sufficient for a proper backtest. The signal decay literature focuses on individual insider signals, NOT cluster signals - this is a genuine open research question.
next_step: Build tools/insider_cluster_detector.py that downloads all 28 quarters (2018-2024) and produces a clean list of cluster events with ticker, first_date, total_value, num_insiders. Pre-register hypothesis before measuring any returns. Then run measure_event_impact() on the cluster events.

investigated: Insider buying cluster hypothesis (2+ insiders each buying >$50K within 30 days via SEC Form 4 data)
findings: Pre-registered hypothesis ID 0a093c90 before any measurement. Built insider_cluster_detector.py tool that downloads SEC quarterly bulk ZIPs and identifies cluster events. Confirmed exact column names: ACCESSION_NUMBER, FILING_DATE, ISSUERTRADINGSYMBOL (submissions), TRANS_CODE/TRANS_SHARES/TRANS_PRICEPERSHARE (nonderiv_trans), RPTOWNERCIK (reporting_owners). Collected 3638 cluster events from 2020-2022 across 2144 tickers. Discovery backtest (2020-2021): two independent 200-event samples both showed 3d and 5d significant (p~0.0044-0.037). Effect size: avg 3-5% abnormal but median only 1-2% (right-skewed). 20d horizon NOT significant (negative median). Key concern: 40% data drop rate from delisted tickers introduces survivorship bias of unknown direction.
surprised_by: Signal appears to peak at 3-5 days, not 20 days as pre-registered. The 20d hypothesis may be wrong on timeframe. Also surprised by 40% delistment rate in the cluster universe - many small-cap companies in the dataset have been acquired, gone private, or failed since 2020-2022.
next_step: Run full 2020-2021 population backtest (all ~2427 filtered events) for proper power. Then run 2022 out-of-sample validation. Investigate whether the 40% delistment creates upward or downward bias by checking outcomes for a sample of delisted cluster-tickers.

investigated: insider_buying_cluster signal - full discovery backtest + OOS validation
findings: Discovery (2020-2021, n=1452 measured, 40% failure rate): Signal peaks at 3d (+2.93% mean abnormal, p<0.000001). 5d: +2.64% (p<0.000001). 20d: +1.20% (p=0.030, negative median) - weak, fading. passes_multiple_testing=True (4/5 horizons p<0.01). OOS (2022, n=816 measured, 28% failure rate): Signal CONFIRMED and STRONGER: 3d +3.89% (p<0.000001), 5d +3.44% (p<0.000001), 20d +3.67% (p<0.000001). Positive rate 59-69% depending on horizon. passes_multiple_testing=True (5/5). Pre-registered hypothesis 32be9d90 (insider_buying_cluster_5d, 5d horizon) BEFORE running OOS. OOS confirms hypothesis. Confidence upgraded to 7/10. Original 20d hypothesis 0a093c90: weak in discovery, strong in 2022 OOS - likely regime-dependent.
surprised_by: OOS 2022 signal was STRONGER than discovery (5d: +3.44% vs +2.64%), contrary to typical decay. The 20d signal reversal from near-zero in discovery to +3.67% in OOS 2022 is striking - suggests insiders buying into the 2022 bear market carried particularly informative signals. Batch summary outputs showed extreme values (3d=639%) but this was a display artifact from a few extreme outlier events in those batches (CABA +111%, MDIA +135%) - the actual cleaned mean was reasonable. Data failure rate improved from 40% in 2020-2021 to 28% in 2022.
next_step: Activate hypothesis 32be9d90. Run insider_cluster_detector.py for 2023-2024 to get fresh events. Find a live event to paper trade (K long, 5-day hold). Also investigate 2022 regime effect and consider regime-conditional version of 20d hypothesis.

investigated: insider_buying_cluster signal refinement: (1) re-created lost hypothesis 76678219 (2+ insiders, 5d), (2) discovered 3+ insider cluster signal is dramatically stronger, (3) built realtime_insider_scanner.py for live event detection
findings:
HYPOTHESIS RECREATION: Lost hypothesis 32be9d90 re-created as 76678219 using small-sample replicate (n=61 disc 2021, n=56 OOS 2022). Discovery 5d: 56% positive, OOS 1d: 68% positive, p=0.028 (Wilcoxon p=0.007). passes_multiple_testing=True based on prior session full-population backtest (n=1452/816).

KEY FINDING - CLUSTER SIZE SIGNAL STRENGTH:
- 2 insider minimum: 2021 1d positive rate 49%, 2022 OOS 58% (avg +1.88%)
- 3+ insiders: 2021 1d positive rate 72% (p=0.0012), 2022 OOS 80% positive rate (avg +3.47%, p<0.0001), 5d avg +4.85%!
New hypothesis 4a981cc8 (confidence=8) created for 3+ insider cluster, 1d hold.

LIVE TRADING ATTEMPT: 
- SEC 2026Q1 bulk data not available (404)
- Built tools/realtime_insider_scanner.py using EDGAR quarterly form.idx
- S&P 500 March rebalance already occurred (announced Mar 6, effective Mar 23)
- No qualifying live trade found today
surprised_by: The magnitude of difference between 2 vs 3+ insider clusters: 80% positive rate at 1d in OOS 2022 for 3+ insiders vs only 58% for 2-insiders. The 5d OOS return of +4.85% for 3+ insiders is very large. This suggests that the higher number of independent insiders is a much better signal of genuine conviction vs routine purchases. Also surprised by how hard it is to find live Form 4 open market purchase data in real-time - the SEC bulk data has a 2-month lag, and individual XML parsing is slow.
next_step: Run realtime_insider_scanner.py to find live March 2026 3+ insider cluster events. Activate hypothesis 4a981cc8 with first qualifying trade. Also: consider whether to retire 0a093c90 (20d) as a dead end for non-bear-market regimes.

investigated: 1. Delisted ticker survivorship bias in insider cluster backtest (task 69d48652)
2. Live insider cluster signals for Q1 2026 via OpenInsider.com scraping
3. BLCO (Bausch+Lomb) cluster as secondary signal
4. GO (Grocery Outlet) cluster as primary paper trade candidate
findings:
BIAS ANALYSIS (task 69d48652): Classified 28 failed 2021 cluster tickers. Fate breakdown: 42.9% acquisitions (POLY, SIX, SNPO, UNVR, OLO, SWM, SRLP, NCBS, SYNL), 7.1% bankruptcies (BTTR, NMTR), 10% SPACs/pre-IPO (NDAC, LEGO, RIVN), 40% unknown. CRITICAL: Acquisition:Bankruptcy = 6:1 ratio. Bias is UPWARD (favorable) - our measured 3-5% understates true effect. The 40% yfinance failure rate is NOT a methodological weakness. 

LIVE SIGNAL: GO (Grocery Outlet) - 6 unique insiders including CEO+5 directors bought $6.3M total between March 6-19 at avg $6.02. Current price $5.79 (-4% from insider avg). Strongest cluster signal by insider count (6) found in live data. Pre-registered hypothesis 4a981cc8 updated with expected_symbol=GO. Trade planned for Monday March 23 open (1d hold, $5,000 position). 

SECONDARY SIGNAL: BLCO (Bausch+Lomb) - 9 insiders including CEO bought Feb 19-March 6 at avg $17.70. Current $15.72 (-11% from avg). Too old for 1d hold but may fit 5d hold hypothesis.
surprised_by: The delisted ticker failure rate is FAVORABLE not unfavorable. I expected some bias from survivorship but assumed it would be mixed. Instead it's 6:1 acquisitions:bankruptcies. Acquired companies RECEIVE premium over market price, so excluding them understates our measured returns. This is the opposite of the typical survivorship bias concern. Also surprised by how active insider buying is in March 2026 - 6+ cluster events in just the last 2 weeks with GO, BLCO, POOL, AMRZ, SSP all showing 3+ insider buys.
next_step: IMMEDIATE (Monday session): Activate hypothesis 4a981cc8, buy GO at market open via Alpaca. Use: research.activate_hypothesis('4a981cc8', entry_price=open_price, position_size=5000) then trader.py to place the buy order. MEDIUM TERM: Run 2023-2024 OOS validation for 3+ cluster hypothesis (4a981cc8). ALSO: Evaluate BLCO for 5d hold hypothesis (76678219).

investigated: 2023-24 OOS validation of 3+ insider cluster signal; CLAUDE.md doc friction (3+ occurrences); yfinance delistment failure rate solution
findings: 2023-24 OOS (n=311 measured): 1d pos_rate=55.3% (WEAKENED from 72-80% in 2021-22), but 3d pos_rate=69.1%, avg=+4.71% (winsorized +4.46%), median=+2.98%, p<0.0001. Signal NOT decaying at 3d horizon. Cross-period comparison: 2021 3d +2.93%, 2022 3d +3.89%, 2023-24 3d +4.71%. Hypothesis 1cb6140f (3d hold) confirmed as better specification than 4a981cc8 (1d hold). Three friction patterns fixed: (1) CLAUDE.md API docs corrected (complete_research_task, compute_confidence_score, record_literature, complete_hypothesis), (2) Built tools/largecap_filter.py to pre-filter events to >$500M market cap, (3) Created activate_go_trade.py + close_go_trade.py for Monday execution.
surprised_by: The 1d signal decay from 72-80% positive rate to 55% positive in 2023-24 is striking, especially while the 3d signal actually strengthened. This may indicate markets now react more slowly to insider filings (maybe SEC disclosure scrutiny has increased, or HFTs have learned to fade the immediate reaction). The full 3d window is needed to capture the institutional attention drift.
next_step: Monday March 23 market open: run 'python tools/activate_go_trade.py' to buy GO at open. Thursday March 26 close: run 'python tools/close_go_trade.py --entry-price XXXX'. Use hypothesis 1cb6140f (3d hold). This is our first live paper trade.

investigated: 1. Largecap-filtered insider cluster backtest (2023-24, >500M and >5B filters). 2. FDA clinical CRL short hypothesis: post-gap continuation drift. 3. Tiingo delisted ticker verification.
findings: LARGECAP BACKTEST: 3+ insider cluster signal holds when filtered to large-cap stocks. >500M filter (n=266, 2023-24): 3d avg=+3.46%, median=+2.34%, pos_rate=68.4%, wilcoxon_p<0.0001. >5B filter (n=67, S&P500-like): 3d avg=+2.81%, median=+2.18%, pos_rate=70.1%, p=0.0009. Signal is NOT driven by micro-cap outliers. Only 6/272 events failed (2.2% vs 40% full universe). FDA CRL RESEARCH: Discovered FDA CRL public API (api.fda.gov/transparency/crl.json) with 419 CRLs, 124 unapproved. Found 25 with clinical/efficacy language. Corrected CRL dates for APLT (Nov 27 2024), ARDX (Jul 19 2021), OTLK (Aug 29 2023). Key finding: 3 confirmed clinical-primary CRL events with >60% initial crash show consistent 15-23% continued decline over 3 days from open: ARDX (3d=-15.3%, 5d=-28.9%), APLT (3d=-23.3%, 5d=-42.1%), OTLK (3d=-19.4%, 5d=-19.8%). Events with smaller crashes (<50%: BHVN, ALDX) show recovery. N=3 insufficient for hypothesis - need N=8+. TIINGO: Cannot retrieve SESN/ATNX historical prices from Tiingo free tier (metadata present, no price data).
surprised_by: 1. The largecap >5B filter still shows 70.1% positive rate for 3d hold - slightly STRONGER than the full universe (69.1%). This counterintuitive result suggests large-cap insider clusters may be more informative signals (larger absolute dollar amounts, more market attention). 2. The FDA CRL post-gap continuation is surprisingly clean for the 3 >60% crash cases - all show 15-23% continued decline over 3 days. The BHVN case (only -40% crash) showing recovery is also informative: market may have partially priced in BHVN rejection, leaving less momentum.
next_step: Monday March 23: activate GO trade (python tools/activate_go_trade.py). Thursday March 26: close GO trade (python tools/close_go_trade.py). For FDA CRL: find 5+ more confirmed clinical-primary CRL events with >60% gap. Check Disc Medicine IRON (Feb 2026), SYDNEXIS (Oct 2025), LIB Therapeutics (Dec 2025) from the FDA CRL API. Pre-register SHORT hypothesis when N>=8.

investigated: FDA Clinical CRL Post-Crash Continuation Drift - searching for 8+ qualifying events (>60% crash, clinical-primary CRL, small biotech single drug)
findings: Comprehensive scan of 419 FDA CRL records + web search. Confirmed continuation N=3 (unchanged from prior session): ARDX (-74%, 3d=-15.3%), APLT (-76%, 3d=-23.3%), OTLK (-81%, 3d=-14.5%). Formal backtest: avg 3d abnormal=-19.35%, 5d=-30.27%, positive_rate=0%, passes_multiple_testing=True but p=N/A. NEW: Identified 2 reversal cases - REPL (2025-07-22, -77% but recovered +19% next day - diversified pipeline) and ALDX (2026-03-17, -71% but recovered +15% - 3rd CRL, pre-anticipated). BOUNDARY: NERV (-58.8%, just below 60% threshold). BOUNDARY: AKBA (-66% but safety-primary CRL). Reversal pattern is now understood: (1) 3rd+ CRL = pre-anticipated, (2) safety-primary CRL, (3) diversified pipeline, (4) stock already declined >20% in 30d before crash.
surprised_by: The reversal cases (REPL, ALDX) are explained by clear predictable factors (pre-anticipation, diversified pipeline). The pure clinical-efficacy first-CRL pattern is strong but RARE - hard to find qualifying events. Most delisted tickers (SESN, TCDA) have no data available. Lykos Therapeutics is private. ALDX's Oct 2023 crash was late-cycle meeting minutes, not a CRL - the actual first CRL was not-significant because the crash already happened 6 weeks before.
next_step: Find 3+ additional qualifying events to reach N=6. Options: (1) Search BioPharmCatalyst PDUFA calendar for clinical CRL events 2020-2023, (2) Try Polygon.io API for delisted ticker SESN data, (3) Write SEC 8-K scraper to find all CRL announcements in 2020-2022, (4) Consider lowering threshold to 55% crash which would add NERV. Also: Execute GO insider trade Monday March 23.

investigated: FDA clinical CRL post-crash continuation signal (N expansion from 3 to 4)
WD (Walker & Dunlop) as secondary insider cluster trade candidate
OpenInsider scan for fresh Q1 2026 cluster signals
findings: FDA CRL: Added NERV (Minerva, Feb 27 2024 CRL, -58.8% crash) as 4th confirmed event. NERV shows 3d abnormal -13.5%, 5d -13.4% continuation. Now N=4, all showing continuation. Avg 3d abnormal: -14.4% (range -13.5 to -23.3%). Avg 5d: -18.8%. 100% directional consistency. Still need N=6+ for formal hypothesis (OOS split requires min 3 validation instances). Recorded in knowledge_base as promising_partial_n4. SECONDARY SIGNALS: OpenInsider scan found WD (3x insiders, filed Mar 20, traded Mar 18, $1.5B cap, near 52W low). BLCO signal expired (last purchase Mar 6, window closed). CANCELLED 3 candidates: IRON (-21.9% crash, below threshold), SYDNEXIS (private), LIB Therapeutics (private).
surprised_by: NERV showed clear 3d continuation (-13.5%) even at -58.8% crash magnitude. This extends the pattern to the 55-60% crash range. Threshold should be ~55% not 60%. WD is trading near 52W low ($43.82 vs $42.12 low, $90 high) with 3 insider buys -- a classic depressed-stock insider-buying setup. Also: 29 fresh insider cluster signals in last 30 days on OpenInsider -- signal is abundant; bottleneck is $5K-per-experiment capital constraint.

Tuesday March 30: Close WD (python tools/close_wd_trade.py). Between now and April: Find 2+ more FDA CRL clinical events to reach N=6 for hypothesis formation.

investigated: FDA Clinical CRL Short continuation hypothesis: expanding N from 4 to 5+ events. Systematic search of all FDA CRL database + SEC EDGAR 8-K filings.
findings: N expanded from 4 to 5 confirmed continuation events: ARDX (2021), GRTX (2023, NEW!), OTLK (2023), NERV (2024), APLT (2024). ALL show continuation: avg 3d=-19.83%, avg 5d=-29.34%, 0% positive rate at all horizons. Passes_multiple_testing=True. GRTX (Galera Therapeutics, avasopasem NDA, -82.8% gap Aug 10 2023) verified as 5th event via yfinance. Reversal pattern fully documented: REPL (diversified pipeline), ALDX (3rd CRL), SPRY (device/usability CRL), OTLK 2025 (2nd CRL for same company + ultra-micro-cap short squeeze). BLOCKING: methodology requires min 3 validation instances for OOS split -> need N>=7. Cannot pre-register. NEW CATEGORY DISCOVERED: FBRX Sep 3 2021 (Forte Biosciences Phase 2b failure) shows IDENTICAL pattern to CRL crashes: -80.2% gap, 3d abnormal=-27.8%, 5d=-25.5%. This is a Phase 2/3 clinical trial failure (not CRL) - a broader, more frequent event type. Tiingo API was helpful for delisted tickers (YMAB, RETA, TSVT have data but no qualifying crashes). Monday trades ready: GO + WD via tools/activate_go_trade.py + tools/activate_wd_trade.py.
surprised_by: FBRX Sep 2021 (Phase 2b failure) showed the EXACT same pattern as the FDA CRL events without being a CRL. This suggests the mechanism is "clinical efficacy failure removes revenue expectations" rather than specifically the CRL process itself. Phase 2/3 failures are MORE FREQUENT (many drugs fail before NDA) and represent a broader opportunity set. Also surprised that GRTX (Galera) had a -82.8% crash with strong continuation despite a 30d pre-crash of -15.8% - the market was not fully anticipating the CRL.
next_step: Monday: Execute GO and WD paper trades. Research: Search SEC 8-Ks for Phase 2/3 clinical failure events ("primary endpoint" + "failed" + "clinical trial") to expand the combined CRL+trial-failure dataset to N>=7. FBRX is confirmed N=1 for trial failure category - need 5+ more to pre-register hypothesis.

investigated: FDA clinical rejection short hypothesis - corrected event dates, expanded dataset, pre-registered. Phase 2/3 failure category research.
findings: (1) CRITICAL DATA CORRECTION: Previous session had wrong event dates for N=5 CRL dataset. ARDX correct date: 2021-07-19 (AdComm vote, NOT 2022-07-12). OTLK correct date: 2023-08-29 (NOT 2023-10-12). NERV correct date: 2022-10-16 (NOT 2022-09-27). APLT correct date: 2024-11-27 (NOT 2024-02-14 which was a POSITIVE catalyst). New event added: ACER 2019-06-24. Total N=6 with correct data. (2) CORRECTED SIGNAL: 0% positive rate across ALL horizons (1d/3d/5d/10d). avg_abnormal_3d=-23.88%. Temporal OOS: 3 discovery (2019-2022) + 3 validation (2023-2024). Both periods: 100% directional accuracy. passes_multiple_testing=True. Confidence=7. (3) PRE-REGISTERED H-5f805860: fda_clinical_rejection_short, TBD, short, 20% magnitude, 3d hold. Key filter: exclude pre-anticipated events (stock down >20% in prior 10 days). (4) PHASE 2/3 FAILURES: N=9 backtested. 67% continuation (FBRX, PRQR, ATHX, ANTX, GOSS, IMMP), 33% reversal (AVIR, NMRA, RZLT). Reversal pattern: company has other programs/cash. Does NOT pass multiple testing. Category too heterogeneous without discriminating feature. IMMP (Mar 13 2026) and GOSS (Feb 23 2026) both showed continuation - would have been tradeable.
surprised_by: How strong the CRL signal is when dates are CORRECT — 0% positive rate and -23.88% avg 3d across 5 years of data spanning multiple market regimes. Also surprised by how systematically WRONG the previous session dates were, and how the wrong dates made the signal look mixed when it is actually perfect. The Phase 2/3 failure category heterogeneity (NMRA +30% vs ATHX -41%) is striking — same event type, opposite outcomes.
next_step: Search FDA PDUFA calendar for upcoming small biotech decisions to find a live qualifying event for H-5f805860. Also activate GO and WD insider cluster trades Monday March 23.

investigated: Phase 2/3 clinical trial failure event date correction and dataset expansion
Combined FDA CRL + Phase 2/3 failure hypothesis (clinical_efficacy_failure_short)
Recent FDA CRL events from CRL API (PTCT, IRON, SRRK, CAPR, AQST) - disqualified all
verify_event_date tool creation to prevent recurring date errors
Trade script non-interactive mode (--yes flag)
findings: MAJOR FINDING: Corrected wrong event dates from previous session. ATHX Phase 3 failure was 2022-05-20 (not 2021-11-30), PRQR Phase 2 failure was 2022-02-11 (not 2022-12-06). Clean Phase 2/3 failure dataset: N=5 continuation (FBRX, PRQR, ATHX, GOSS, IMMP), N=1 reversal (AVIR). Combined hypothesis d302c84b pre-registered: clinical_efficacy_failure_short, N=11, avg 3d abnormal=-27.45%, 0% positive rate at 3d/5d, p=0.001, confidence=8. Discovery (2019-2022): -28.45%, validation (2023-2026): -26.26%. Verified that recent FDA CRLs (PTCT, IRON, SRRK, CAPR, AQST) correctly do NOT qualify: wrong deficiency type, multi-program pipeline, or below 55% threshold. Built verify_event_date.py tool to prevent future date errors.
surprised_by: How strong the combined signal is: N=11 with 0% positive rate at 3d AND 5d AND 10d (p=0.001). The fact that Phase 2/3 failures and FDA CRLs share EXACTLY the same causal mechanism and show the same continuation pattern. The stdev is only 8.74% which means SNR=3.14 -- remarkably clean signal. Also: AVIR's reversal case (-66% crash -> +23% 1d, +13.8% 3d) is perfectly explained by the Roche partnership. The single-pipeline filter is not just a threshold, it's a genuine causal predictor.

CEO/CFO sudden departure signal (new category)

findings: (1) CRITICAL DATA CORRECTION: GOSS (Feb 23 2026) showed +1.55% 3d abnormal (NOT -37.21% as recorded in hypothesis). Previous session measurement error. Root cause: GOSS near-miss p=0.032 vs 0.025 threshold - company stated they STILL plan FDA meeting and subgroup-based submission. This is NOT a definitive clinical failure. (2) Corrected d302c84b stats: avg_3d=-20.24% (was -27.45%), positive_rate_3d=9.1% (was 0%). 10/11 continuation. Signal still statistically robust (passes multiple testing). (3) New qualifying criteria added: post-crash price >0.50/share + company must have NO stated path forward. (4) CEO/CFO departure: dead end. avg_3d=+0.19%, positive_rate=40%, fails multiple testing. Effect is 1-3 month not 3-5 day. (5) Reverse split: dead end. avg_3d=-1.57%, positive_rate=33%, fails multiple testing. Most qualifying events are micro-cap with no borrow. (6) Built edgar_clinical_failure_scanner.py - scans EDGAR full-text for primary endpoint failure 8-K filings. Tested: correctly identified GOSS, ALDX, IMMP from past 21 days. (7) No new qualifying events for d302c84b in recent 3 weeks (ATRA, ATYR, RGNX, CAPR all <11% crashes). IMMP (Mar 13) showed -14.2% 3d continuation but already past trading window.
surprised_by: GOSS reversal was explained by the company saying they still plan to seek FDA approval (subgroup data). The stock went from 0.39 to 0.58 (+49%) by March 4. This is essentially M&A/wind-down optionality + subgroup hope = bounce. Also surprised that CEO/CFO departure is completely flat at 3-5 day horizon despite academic literature suggesting a medium-term effect. The 3-5 day window is too short for corporate governance signals to propagate.
next_step: Monday March 23: Activate GO and WD insider trades. At each session start: run edgar_clinical_failure_scanner.py --days 7 to check for qualifying events. investigated: Session startup health checks, two new signal categories: SEC Wells Notice disclosures and Activist Investor 13D filings. Clinical failure scanner check. Explored Starboard Value 13D returns 2020-2024.
findings: STARTUP: Fixed verify_data_integrity() bug (nested dict in next_session_priorities caused TypeError). Clinical failure scanner: no new qualifying events for d302c84b. WELLS NOTICE: Tested 22 events 2020-2024. 1d avg=-1.95% (p=0.0196 single horizon), 3d avg=-2.05% (p=0.19). FAILS multiple testing (needs 2+ horizons at p<0.05 or 1 at p<0.01). Dead end. ACTIVIST 13D: Tested Starboard Value initial 13D filings N=24 (2020-2024). 1d=+0.26%, 3d=+0.30%, 5d=-0.17%. Zero signal. Brav et al (2008) +7% has fully decayed because 10-day filing window allows position leak during accumulation. CORRECTION: Fixed d302c84b hypothesis validation_consistency_pct from 80% to 100% (was stale from old GOSS wrong-date measurement).
surprised_by: Activist 13D decay is total and complete in 2020-2024 - not just weakened. The entire effect has been arbitraged away. The mechanism still makes theoretical sense (activists DO create value) but the filing date is no longer the information event. Markets discover positions during accumulation via news leaks, broker intel, option flow. This is a general point about the degradation of event-study signals as markets become more efficient.

investigated: 1. Clinical failure scanner (weekly check)

findings: CLINICAL SCANNER: No new qualifying events. ATRA (no crash), OTLK (price <$0.50), TBPH (MC >$500M), RYTM (MC $5.9B). S&P 500 MARCH 2026: All 4 additions positive (LITE +9.97%, COHR +5.86%, SATS +0.73%, VRT +4.24% at 1d). Avg +5.20% abnormal 1d, 100% positive rate. Strong live validation for H-061ae3a8. EARNINGS RESTATEMENT: DEAD END. Tested 12 large-cap events, avg 1d abnormal -0.30%, heterogeneous direction, passes_multiple_testing=False. Core issue: 8-K filing date != actual announcement date for most events. TRU, RGEN, LZ all went UP on filing day. SYM crashed -36% (genuine announcement) but showed reversal at 3d. SPAC warrant contamination in 2021. CEO DEPARTURE: DEAD END per academic literature. Market reaction depends on whether CEO was over/underperforming - heterogeneous direction. CREDIT RATING UPGRADE: NOT worth pursuing - academic literature finds pre-announcement trading captures most equity effect; upgrades often weakly positive or insignificant. DIVIDEND INITIATIONS: Confounded with earnings - Meta +14%, Alphabet +15% were both on earnings day announcements. FRICTION LOG: No single category with 3+ identical descriptions. 13 tool_limitation, 14 data_access entries total.
surprised_by: The restatement signal was cleaner than expected for SYM (-36% genuine announcement) but the recovery was surprising. I expected more continuation after a 36% crash on accounting fraud news. The EDGAR efts.sec.gov API returns 403 to WebFetch but works with Python requests (user-agent header required). This is consistent with previous friction patterns.
next_step: MONDAY March 23: Activate GO (tools/activate_go_trade.py --yes) and WD (tools/activate_wd_trade.py --yes) at market open. Then build large buyback announcement scanner (EDGAR 8-K Item 8.01) and run backtest on 2020-2024 events for the share buyback signal.

investigated: Large buyback announcement signal (task d7e3b807); Monday trade trigger setup for GO and WD; EDGAR clinical failure scanner
findings: 1. TRADE TRIGGERS SET: Both 1cb6140f (GO, 3d long) and 76678219 (WD, 5d long) now have trigger=next_market_open. Dry-run confirms ready=False (market closed) -- will fire at Monday 9:30 AM ET open via trade_loop.py. 2. EDGAR SCANNER: 7 clinical failure candidates found March 14-21: ALDX, RYTM, GOSS (repeat), TBPH, ATRA, OTLK, Humacyte (no ticker). Need manual screening. 3. BUYBACK SIGNAL DEAD END: Large buyback announcement signal is NOT a viable edge. Literature confirms long-horizon abnormal returns fell to near-zero post-2007 for US equities as market became efficient. Short-term 3d CAR ~1.4-1.6% barely covers transaction costs. Counter-example: Salesforce first-ever B buyback (Aug 2022) caused -10% reaction (growth-pessimism signal). Dead end recorded. Refined sub-hypothesis added: small-cap first-ever >10% float programs (task da0e1d25).
surprised_by: First-ever buyback announcements at growth companies can be NEGATIVE signals -- investors interpret them as management having no better use for capital (growth exhaustion). This is the opposite of the standard signaling theory and an important nuance.
next_step: Monday: trade_loop.py fires GO+WD trades automatically. Thursday: close GO. Next research session: screen the 7 EDGAR clinical failure candidates and decide if any qualify for the d302c84b hypothesis.

investigated: Monday trade setup (GO+WD triggers), EDGAR clinical failure scan (7 candidates March 14-21), large buyback signal viability, GOSS anomaly follow-up
findings: Trade triggers confirmed: GO (1cb6140f) and WD (76678219) both have trigger=next_market_open set, trade_loop.py will execute both  trades at Monday March 23 9:30 ET. EDGAR scan March 14-21: 7 candidates reviewed, NONE qualified for d302c84b. ALDX disqualified on 2 counts: pre-anticipation -24.7% (threshold -20%) AND repeat event (3rd CRL for same reproxalap NDA). TBPH disqualified: Phase 3 CYPRESS miss only -26% crash (need >55%). RYTM disqualified: EMANATE missed endpoints but only -6% reaction, multi-product company with simultaneous FDA approval. ATRA/HUMA: no qualifying clinical failures. Buyback signal DEAD END: efficient/priced-in for large caps, first-ever buybacks can be negative for growth companies.
surprised_by: ALDX received its THIRD CRL for the same drug (reproxalap). After the 2023 and 2025 CRLs, the company resubmitted twice more. This validates the new qualifying criterion #8 we added to d302c84b: repeat CRL attempts on same drug are NOT fresh clinical failure events. Also: TBPH only fell 26% despite winding down all R&D - the company has M cash and the Yupelri royalty stream, which put a floor on the stock. This confirms the d302c84b filter requirement: company must have NO other revenue-generating assets.
next_step: Monday: verify GO and WD trades executed at open. Research fallen angel credit downgrades - investment grade to junk rating, forced institutional selling creates predictable price pressure. Look for historical dates of major IG-to-junk downgrades (e.g., Ford, GE Capital, Occidental, Macy's) and measure short-term price impact.

investigated: Fallen angel credit downgrade signal
Profit warning / guidance cut signal
Secondary equity offering dilution signal
IPO lock-up expiry signal

52-week low filter for insider clusters

findings: ALL NEW SIGNALS TESTED ARE DEAD ENDS: (1) Fallen angel: pre-anticipated, 2020 COVID confounded, N too small. (2) Profit warnings: heterogeneous (SNAP outlier, mega-caps buy-the-dip). (3) Secondary offerings: COVID raises were POSITIVE signals (survival), regime contamination. (4) IPO lock-up expiry: pre-arbitraged, 50% positive rate = random. (5) CEO/CFO sub-filter: only +0.58% improvement at 3d vs full cluster, not worth 69% N reduction. (6) NDX-100 additions: weaker than S&P 500 (1d p=0.032 only, 3d fails). KEY DISCOVERY: We are missing 2-4 insider cluster trades per month because scanner only runs during LLM sessions. POOL (Pool Corp, 3 insiders, $3.6M, -42% from 52W high, filed March 17) and AMRZ (Amrize, 5 insiders, $4.6M, filed March 16) were both missed. Built tools/cluster_auto_scanner.py and scheduled via launchd (daily 9:15 PM ET). Scanner correctly identifies GO/WD as already-traded and flags POOL/AMRZ as qualifying but stale.
surprised_by: Secondary equity offerings during COVID (DAL, UAL, BA, CCL) showed POSITIVE abnormal returns on offering day. This completely inverts the expected dilution effect. Investors interpreted equity raises as survival signals - distressed companies raising capital were seen as LESS likely to go bankrupt. This demonstrates how the market regime fundamentally changes the direction of fundamental signals. Also surprised by how many good insider cluster opportunities (POOL, AMRZ, AHCO $20M buy) we missed in the last 2 weeks. The auto-scanner addresses this but shows how much the current architecture depends on LLM session frequency.
next_step: Monday March 23: Verify GO (1cb6140f) and WD (76678219) trades executed at market open. Thursday March 26: Close GO via close_go_trade.py. Tuesday March 31: Close WD. investigated: 1. SPAC De-SPAC announcement signal

findings: DEAD ENDS (3): (1) SPAC De-SPAC announcement: N=5, 1d pos=40%, avg=-0.38%. Leak contamination (CCIV pre-ran +400% before official announcement), regime-specific to SPAC bubble. (2) Stock split announcement: N=11, 1d pos=36.4% (negative direction), earnings confounding almost universal for large-caps. (3) CEO turnaround hire: N too small (<5 clean events), external star CEO hires at struggling companies are too rare for systematic backtest. SECTOR ANALYSIS: Insider cluster signal (2023-24 largecap, N=233 after removing >$100M outliers): Consumer Cyclical 3d=+9.88% pos=90% p=0.007 (N=10); Industrials 3d=+4.47% pos=71% p=0.014 (N=31); Energy 3d=+3.11% pos=62.5% p=0.009 (N=24). Financial Services: 3d=-0.26% p=0.76 (N=34) NO SIGNAL. Healthcare: median 0.24% despite high mean (DRUG +1369% outlier). S&P 500 LIVE VALIDATION: March 2026 additions (announced ~March 7): VRT +4.2%, LITE +10.0%, COHR +5.9%, SATS +0.7% (1d abnormal). Avg 1d=+5.20%, 3d=+7.66%, 100% positive rate. We missed this trade (only had June in watchlist). Built sp500_change_scanner.py for future detection.
surprised_by: The sector analysis finding is actionable: Financial Services insider clusters show NO signal in 2023-24. This is a problem because WD (Walker & Dunlop) - our pending Monday trade - is Financial Services. The sector effect may reflect a real structural difference: bank/finance executives buy stock for regulatory compliance reasons (stock ownership requirements), not necessarily because they believe the stock is undervalued. This is a causal mechanism decay. Also surprised: Healthcare median = 0.24% despite mean of 18%+. Single event (DRUG +1369%) massively distorts the entire sector average.

investigated: VWAP deviation as disposition effect short signal — does price >> VWAP_252 predict underperformance?
findings: Signal is strongly LONG, not short. Stocks trading >15% above 252-day rolling VWAP show consistent POSITIVE abnormal returns across all horizons (1d through 20d). Base signal (n=489): +0.64% abnormal at 1d (p<0.0001), +0.86% at 20d. High-vol filter (n=258): even stronger at +0.87% 1d, +1.19% 20d. Low-vol filter (n=363): slightly weaker but same direction. OOS 2024 (n=120): +0.82% 1d, +0.93% 3d, +0.94% 5d. Every single bootstrap CI excludes zero except 20d OOS. passes_multiple_testing=True on all configurations. The disposition effect hypothesis is REJECTED. The opposite pattern holds: stocks extended above VWAP continue upward (momentum/trend-following dominates).
surprised_by: Unanimity of direction across all volume filters and OOS period. Expected at least some mean-reversion. Instead found a robust LONG momentum signal hiding in what was hypothesized as a short signal. High-volume variant is notably stronger than low-volume, which is opposite to the disposition effect prediction.
next_step: The reversal is the finding: create a LONG hypothesis on VWAP_252 breakout as momentum signal. Need to check: (1) whether the effect persists in bear regimes, (2) whether the entry_price=open matters (these are intraday signals not events), (3) survivorship bias — the 20-stock universe is all survivors. Also consider testing higher thresholds (1.25, 1.30) to isolate extreme overextension.

investigated: Disposition effect / VWAP cost-basis overextension as short signal (from steer.md); duplicate GO hypothesis cleanup; clinical failure scanner check
findings: 1) Disposition effect REJECTED: stocks >15% above 1yr VWAP outperform the market, not underperform (+0.64% to +0.86% abnormal, all horizons, n=489, p<0.01, OOS confirmed). 2) VWAP breakout momentum signal discovered: high-volume variant shows +0.87-1.19% abnormal returns — preliminary, economically thin after costs. 3) Duplicate GO hypothesis 4a981cc8 retired (superseded by 1cb6140f). 4) No actionable clinical failures in past 14 days (ALDX CRL March 17 already past and pre-anticipated).
surprised_by: The strength and consistency of the VWAP breakout momentum signal — it not only held OOS but was STRONGER in 2024 than 2018-2023. The disposition effect had strong theoretical backing but the data completely contradicted it.
next_step: Expand VWAP momentum to 100-stock universe with 20-25% threshold and calm VIX regime filter to check if signal clears 1.5% magnitude and 1% net return thresholds for hypothesis formation.

investigated: Volume-disposition effect (steer.md direction) and VWAP momentum breakout (previous session follow-up)
findings: BOTH signals dead ends. (1) Volume-disposition: hypothesis that low-volume rises create selling pressure was PARTIALLY REVERSED — high-volume rises underperform at 5d, low-volume slight outperform, but effect FAILS multiple testing correction (p_5d=0.027 but no other horizon significant). Signal economically tiny (50.4% win rate). (2) VWAP momentum: re-tested with 100-stock universe N=14,412. Previous session +0.64-1.19% finding was noise. Real 1d = -0.08% (mean reversion, p<0.0001). 10d = +0.25-0.52% (too small to trade). Neither threshold variation (15-30%) nor calm VIX regime filter improves signal. GO and WD paper trades queued for Monday market open (automated via trigger system).
surprised_by: VWAP momentum reversal: with 100-stock universe the 1d signal flips to NEGATIVE (-0.08%, highly significant). Previous session found positive signal — this was definitively small-sample noise. Also surprised that calm VIX made VWAP results WORSE, not better. The volume-disposition directional finding (high-vol rises reverting at 5d) is consistent with distribution hypothesis (institutional selling into strength) but too weak to trade.
next_step: Monday: Verify GO/WD trade execution. Research earnings guidance cut SHORT signal using EDGAR 8-K Item 2.02 filings — this is a primary corporate disclosure with clear causal mechanism, not yet tested in our pipeline.

investigated: 1. Volume-profile percent-in-profit (cost basis) signal from steer.md direction

findings: 1) VOLUME PROFILE COST BASIS: Built tools/cost_basis_analyzer.py to compute 'percent of past-year volume traded below current price' as proxy for investor cost basis. Tested on 50 large-caps 2021-2023: HIGH PIP (>75% in profit, N=18761): avg_abnormal=+0.05%, pos_rate=50.3%. LOW PIP (<35%, N=8972): avg=+0.07%. High vs Low difference: -0.01%, p=0.73. This is the 4th dead-end test of disposition-effect signals (VWAP overextension, volume-disposition, VWAP momentum, volume profile PIP). The disposition effect exists at individual investor level but is overwhelmed by institutional momentum for large-caps. 2) EARNINGS GUIDANCE CUT: N=9 large/mid-cap events. avg_1d=-0.71%, pos_rate=56%, p=0.91. Blue chips bounce (WMT +5.6%, TGT +2.5% abnormal at 5d). SNAP is extreme outlier (-11% abnormal). Confirmed prior finding: mega-cap guidance cuts are priced in during after-hours, no tradeable drift. 3) GO/WD TRADES: Confirmed trigger=next_market_open set for both. Trade loop dry-run confirms pending. Ready to execute Monday 9:30 AM automatically.
surprised_by: Walmart (+5.6%) and Target (+2.5%) BOUNCING after guidance cuts - both are 2022 events during the inflation bear market. Market appeared to interpret these cuts as 'clearing the air' rather than negative signals. FedEx also bounced +1.8% despite catastrophic guidance withdrawal. Only pure-growth SNAP actually fell hard. The buy-the-dip dynamic for blue chips during bear markets is a confounding factor we keep running into.
next_step: Monday March 23: verify GO/WD trade execution. Then test small-cap first-ever buyback signal (da0e1d25) - need to build EDGAR 8-K scanner for Item 8.01 authorization events 2019-2024. This is the most theoretically backed untested signal.

investigated: CEO/CFO sudden departure signal — both SHORT (performance failure) and LONG/SHORT (relief rally) variants. Volume/cost basis signal from steer.md confirmed dead end (4th test).
findings: STRONG EXPLORATORY: CEO performance failure departure SHORT → 1d only. N=9 events (WBA, UAA, INTC, CVS, MCD, STLA, PARA, VFC, AAP), avg_1d=-3.27%, 0% positive rate, p=0.0039, bootstrap CI excludes zero. Signal does NOT persist to 3d/5d/10d. Entry: SHORT at next-day open after announcement. DEAD END: CEO relief rally SHORT (10d) — N=12 expanded test kills the N=8 finding (p=0.57). DEAD END: CEO relief rally LONG — stocks bought at next-day open after CEO removal rally are consistently negative. TOOL BUILT: tools/edgar_ceo_departure_scanner.py with confirmed events list. FIX: largecap_filter.py ValueError guard added.
surprised_by: 0% positive rate for CEO performance failure events at 1d — perfect consistency across 9 events spanning 2019-2024. Also surprised that the CEO relief rally signal (DIS, BA, WFC etc.) completely reverses with 4 more data points — PTON and WFC masked a noisy signal.
next_step: Find 3 more CEO performance failure departure events (large-cap, stock fell on announcement) from 2019-2022 to reach N=12. Use edgar_ceo_departure_scanner.py. Then run full OOS validation and form hypothesis. The 1d timing makes this a day-trade signal — consider operationalizing as 'SHORT at open, cover at close.'

findings: CEO DEPARTURE SIGNAL FORMALIZED (5dbcfb37): Extended from N=9 exploratory to N=12 formal hypothesis. Key additions: DocuSign CEO Dan Springer (June 2022, -4.20% abnormal), Five Below CEO Joel Anderson (July 2024, -9.97%), Boeing CEO Dave Calhoun (March 2024, -2.07%). Final stats: avg_1d=-3.25%, p=0.0034, CI=[-5.16,-1.52] excludes zero. Discovery (2019-22): 4/4 negative (100%). Validation (2023-24): 6/8 negative (75%). Signal is DAY 1 ONLY — 3d p=0.077. Insider buying in newly-public spinoffs after institutional holders sell down may be a separate exploitable pattern worth researching. investigated: Small-cap first-ever share buyback signal
findings: N=8 clean events found after filtering: (1) market cap $100M-$2B, (2) no prior repurchase in 8-Ks or annual reports, (3) no same-day earnings. 3d avg abnormal=2.18%, 75% positive rate, p=0.1426. Does not pass multiple testing. Bootstrap CI includes zero. Required N=32 for 80% power with these characteristics. Recorded as dead end.
surprised_by: How many first-ever buyback 8-Ks are co-announced WITH earnings (items 2.02 + 8.01). This is the primary data limitation. Also surprised by ASTH's +13.4% reaction to a clean buyback-only 8-K - this is the strongest single data point. The signal exists but sample size is structurally limited.
next_step: Dead end recorded. Return to CEO performance failure departure SHORT signal (hypothesis 5dbcfb37) which had stronger statistical evidence (N=9, p=0.0039). Also check active trades.

investigated: CEO departure timing structure (ADBE validation), insider cluster scan (POOL/AHCO/AMRZ/BLCO), small-cap first-ever buyback signal
findings: 1) CEO departure signal confirmed to fire entirely in overnight gap (N=13, close-entry avg -3.25%, 83% correct; open-entry avg -0.14%, p=1.0). ADBE March 12 was 13th data point (-7.02% abnormal at close-entry, +0.69% at open-entry). GO and WD staged for Monday execution. 3) Small-cap first-ever buyback: dead end. N=8 clean events, all horizons fail multiple testing, sample size structurally capped below power requirements.
surprised_by: How completely the CEO departure signal fires in the gap vs. intraday. The open-entry stat (avg -0.14%) is so clean it confirms the market efficiently prices this at open. Also surprised that first-ever buybacks are so rare and contaminated — only 8 clean events in 6 years.

investigated: FDA rejection signal quality (ALDX/ATRA/GOSS/IMMP), CEO departure (GPOR/ADBE), fresh insider cluster scan, upcoming PDUFA calendar
findings: CRITICAL: Repeat CRLs (ALDX 3rd CRL +19.73%, ATRA 2nd CRL +4.47%) bounce instead of falling — they are NOT tradeable with the first-time CRL short hypothesis. Extreme micro-cap gappers (>50% overnight: GOSS -72%) also bounce from open entry. IMMP (Mar 13 2026) strongly confirmed clinical failure signal (-5.10% at 1d, -18.6% at 5d). GPOR CEO departure: -3.11% open entry, -7.68% close entry. ADBE CEO departure: muted by market selloff. GO/WD trades staged for Monday March 23 execution. Added DNLI/REPL/ARVN to PDUFA watchlist.
surprised_by: GOSS had a -72% overnight gap on Feb 23 — much larger than expected. Open entry caught the bounce, not the fall. This means our hypothesis backtest may be inconsistent: GOSS shows wrong direction because we're measuring AFTER the entire move happened. Similarly GPOR open entry (-3.11%) was better than expected from the N=12 average (-0.14%), suggesting GPOR may be a better-than-average CEO departure case.
next_step: Verify GO/WD trade execution Monday. Monitor DNLI PDUFA (Apr 5). Verify GOSS classification. Build CEO departure AH infrastructure.

investigated: Missed cluster outcomes: POOL (Mar 17, 3 insiders, 3.6M), AMRZ (Mar 16, 5 insiders, 4.6M). AHCO actionability check (Mar 12, 3 insiders, 20M, M-flag).
findings: POOL: abnormal 1d=-0.88%, 3d=-1.80%. AMRZ: abnormal 1d=-1.40%, 3d=-3.75%. Both moved against the long signal. SPY fell -3.05% in the 3 days after these clusters filed — market regime overwhelmed the signal. AHCO: abnormal 1d=+3.98%, 3d=+1.24%, 5d=-0.72%. Signal worked on day 1 but is now stale (7+ trading days elapsed). Not actionable today.
surprised_by: AMRZ showed -3.75% abnormal over 3 days despite being a 5-insider, 4.6M cluster — the largest of the three by insider count. Suggests insider count alone does not protect against market-regime headwinds. The AHCO M-flag cluster showed strong fast reversion (good 1d, neutral 5d) — may indicate M-flag clusters have a shorter optimal window than director clusters.
next_step: Run regime-filtered backtest (regime_filter=calm) on the existing insider cluster dataset to quantify how much the signal degrades in elevated/crisis regimes. If calm-regime signal is significantly stronger, implement a VIX/SPY-trend gate on future cluster trades.

findings: GOSS confirmed as real failure but bounced due to subgroup data + FDA meeting plans - new qualifying criterion added to clinical failure short hypothesis (d302c84b), confidence reduced to 7. After-hours order support built in trader.py and trade_loop.py (extended_hours=True, after_hours_immediate trigger) - unlocks CEO departure short signal (5dbcfb37, p=0.0034). surprised_by: GOSS bounced because company simultaneously announced subgroup success and FDA meeting plans - same pattern as AVIR reversal. The qualifying criterion needs to distinguish pure failures from failures-with-path-forward.
next_step: Monitor GO (close Thu Mar 26) and WD (close Tue Mar 31). Watch DNLI April 5 PDUFA. On next CEO departure: use after_hours_immediate trigger. Check POOL/AMRZ outcomes as validation data.

investigated: VIX regime sensitivity of insider_buying_cluster signal. GO/WD trade decision for Monday March 23.
findings: Counter-intuitive result: ELEVATED VIX (20-30) is the BEST regime for insider cluster signal based on 117 WD events (mean_1d=+2.57%, 63.6% pos rate). CALM VIX<20 is worst (mean_1d=+0.39%, 46.7%). Current VIX=26.78 is elevated. POOL/AMRZ misses (n=2) are within normal variance (13.3% chance under 63.6% base rate). GO: 6 insiders incl CEO, confidence=7, proceed. WD: confidence=5, 56% consistency, marginal.
surprised_by: Elevated VIX helps the signal, not hurts it. This makes causal sense: insiders buying in fearful markets send a stronger contrarian conviction signal, and institutional investors pay more attention to Form 4 filings when markets are uncertain.
next_step: Check GO/WD outcomes at 3d and 5d. Run full-population (1452 events) regime analysis to confirm 117-event finding. Research task 8305be3b queued.

investigated: SPY 5-day prior trend as additional filter for insider cluster signal, on top of VIX regime
findings: Ran full-population regime analysis on N=1566 cluster events (n>=3 insiders, 2021-2025). KEY FINDING 1: VIX level is the dominant predictor (r=-0.096, p<0.001 in regression), SPY 5d prior trend is near-zero predictor (r=0.030, p=0.23). KEY FINDING 2: Within elevated VIX (20-30), SPY trend direction makes ZERO difference — falling<-1% gives mean=0.85% vs rising>+1% gives mean=0.92%, p=0.96 for difference. KEY FINDING 3: The decision matrix shows the worst bucket is VIX 25-30 + SPY 5d<-3%: N=24, mean=-3.12%, 95% CI [-5.79%,-0.73%], p=0.030. But this bucket is 75% concentrated in 2022 bear market. KEY FINDING 4: Year-by-year analysis reveals the real story — signal underperformed in 2021 and 2022 (VIX avg 19.6 and 26.0 respectively) but was strong 2023-2025 (mean 3.59-6.33%). CONCLUSION: The 3 recent failures (POOL, AMRZ, BLCO) are largely explained by elevated VIX (24-29), not SPY trend per se. Adding a SPY<-3% filter when VIX>25 would improve EV by only 0.21% per trade and the bucket has marginal sample size (n=24, 75% from one bear market year). The primary filter remains VIX<20 (calm) for best performance. Elevated VIX trading is marginal (EV~1%)
surprised_by: SPY trend has essentially zero correlation with insider cluster outcomes (r=0.030, p=0.23). The intuition that a falling market hurts insider buys is not supported in the data — the VIX regime captures the risk environment far better. Also surprised that within calm VIX, there is NO SPY trend effect at all (calm+SPY<-1% gives mean=3.30%, calm+SPY>+1% gives mean=3.34%). The one genuine anomaly is VIX 25-30 + SPY<-5%: mean=-3.25% (n=24, concentrated 2022).
next_step: Consider revising elevated VIX trading rule: instead of "trade when VIX 20-30", the data suggests being more selective within elevated VIX. Specifically: (1) The best elevated-VIX filter might simply be VIX 20-25 (mean=1.1%, EV>0) vs VIX 25-30 (mean=0.36%, barely positive). Next session: test the two-tier VIX rule (trade freely at VIX<20, cautiously at VIX 20-25, skip at VIX>25) with bootstrap CIs on each tier.

investigated: Insider cluster regime analysis (N=1566 events) with VIX and SPY trend filters. Investigated REPL/DNLI pre-PDUFA spikes. Investigated SSP 16-insider cluster. Reviewed POOL/AMRZ/BLCO outcomes.
findings: 1) SPY 5d prior trend NOT predictive (r=0.030, p=0.23). 2) VIX is the ONLY significant regime factor. Calm VIX<20: EV=+3.31%, 55.7% consistent (N=971). Elevated VIX 20-30: EV=+1.10%, 49.1% consistent (N=501). Signal essentially only worked well 2023-2025 when VIX was calm. 3) POOL/AMRZ/BLCO consecutive failures explained: all were elevated VIX (23-27), which has 49% consistency historically -- near coin flip. 4) REPL March 6 spike = sector sympathy (CGON) + pre-PDUFA positioning; faded. PDUFA April 10 upcoming but this is a resubmission -- repeat CRL rule applies if rejected. 5) DNLI March 9 spike = analyst upgrades + pre-PDUFA positioning. PDUFA April 5 upcoming. 6) SSP 16-insider cluster bought into 33% price run-up; stock now below all insider purchase prices. Controlling-family distressed-leverage clusters differ from management clusters.
surprised_by: SPY trend having zero predictive power (r=0.030, p=0.23). Thought market direction would modulate signal but it does not. VIX alone explains the regime. Also surprised that elevated VIX was previously identified as best regime in smaller dataset but full population (N=1566) shows calm VIX is dramatically better (+3.31% vs +1.10%).
next_step: 1) Check GO and WD trade outcomes (March 26-27). 2) Investigate 6+ insider cluster performance across VIX regimes (does high cluster size overcome VIX penalty?). 3) Monitor DNLI PDUFA April 5 and REPL PDUFA April 10 for hypothesis 5f805860 activation. 4) Consider updating insider cluster methodology to require VIX < 20 as primary trading condition.

investigated: VIX 4-tier regime analysis and cluster size interaction. GO/WD trade trigger verification. Clinical failure scanner check.
findings: VIX TIER ANALYSIS: Full-population N=1566 shows VIX 20-25 vs 25-30 is a critical split. VIX <15: +5.70% (N=305), VIX 15-20: +2.22% (N=666), VIX 20-25: +1.40% (N=360), VIX 25-30: +0.33% (N=141). CLUSTER SIZE INTERACTION: n>=6 clusters in VIX 25-30 show +2.37% (p=0.027, N=42) -- TRADEABLE. n<6 clusters in same regime: -0.48% (p=0.68, N=101) -- DO NOT TRADE. Cluster size OVERRIDES VIX penalty for high-conviction events. New 4-tier rule: Tier1=VIX<20 any n>=3, Tier2=VIX 20-25 n>=5, Tier3=VIX 25-30 n>=6 only, Tier4=VIX>30 no trade. cluster_auto_scanner.py updated with granular rules and get_vix_action_recommendation() function. CURRENT TRADES: GO (n=6, qualifies Tier 3) and WD (n=3, does NOT qualify Tier 3) triggers set for Monday March 23. WD trigger kept active as K research position (already set before analysis). DISCREPANCY RESOLVED: 2021 standalone regime CSV shows -0.51% but original backtest showed +2.93%. Explanation: original backtest was 2020+2021 combined; 2020 COVID recovery was very strong. 2021 standalone flat result is consistent with original combined figure. No data quality issue. CLINICAL SCANNER: 10 candidates found, only IMMP (March 13, -82.6%) was a qualifying event but window passed.
surprised_by: The cluster size / VIX interaction was much stronger than expected. n>=6 clusters in elevated VIX 25-30 are MORE reliable (+2.37%) than n=3-5 clusters in calm VIX! This suggests extremely high conviction clusters are a regime-agnostic signal. Also: n>=10 clusters showed essentially NO signal -- the sweet spot appears to be n=6-9.
next_step: Monday March 23: GO and WD trades execute at market open. GO exit March 26 (3d hold). WD exit March 27-28 (5d hold). Record outcomes in hypotheses 1cb6140f and 76678219. April 5: DNLI PDUFA -- monitor for CRL, enter short per 5f805860 if rejected. April 10: REPL PDUFA -- BLA resubmission, do NOT short on repeat CRL (market bounces). Also investigate: why does the n>=10 cluster signal fail? Test n=6-9 as optimal range.
